Question

QUESTION TWO  
a) Kalausi Limited is a fast-moving consumer goods company with branches in 5 locations within Kenya. Due to the recent hike in fuel prices, Kalausi has been left with no option but to commensurately increase the prices of its products. In your role as marketing manager at Kalausi you have been tasked to write a report to explain to the next senior management team THREE alternatives which the organization can employ to avert a price increase. Justify your response. (12 Marks)  
b) In your role as marketing manager at Jasiri Enterprises, a manufacturer of assorted biscuits, discuss FOUR considerations which you would make in the selection of a marketing channel. (8 Marks)

QUESTION THREE  
You are the marketing manager of a medium sized firm manufacturing and selling glass crystal and similar giftware. During the COVID 19 pandemic times have been tough, but with a strong balance sheet you are now considering plans to enter a new market with your existing product range suitably engraved. You have decided to offer graduate giftware to students graduating from colleges and universities and have decided to commission market research. As a marketing graduate yourself, you are reflecting on your marketing information requirements for the new marketing initiative. Explain the stages you would undergo in conducting this research. (20 Marks)

QUESTION FOUR  
Corporate responsibility (CSR) is no longer perceived as an activity that businesses halfheartedly invest their time and effort to appease an organization’s board members and impress customers. Using relevant examples, describe FIVE advantages of corporate social responsibility. (20 Marks)

1. ______________émission de radio passe à 10 heures.  
2. _____________ magazine coûte 3 euros.  
C. Complétez les questions suivantes avec quel, quelle, quels, quelles. (2PTS)  
1. ___________ jour êtes-vous libre ?  
2. ___________ ligne de métro prends-tu ce matin ?

1. Casser les œufs. __________ les œufs !  
2. Verser l’eau doucement. _________ l’eau doucement!

6. Ils (venir) en vacances avec nous. ______________________________

Question

1. A company is reviewing actual performance to budget to see where there are differences. The following standard information is relevant: £ per unit Selling price Direct materials Direct labour Fixed production overheads Variable production overheads Fixed selling costs Variable selling cost Total costs Budgeted sales units 3,000 Actual sales units 3,500 50 –– 4 16 5 10 1 1 –– 37 –– What was the favourable sales volume variance using marginal costing?  
A £9,500  
B £7,500  
C £7,000  
D £6,500

2. A company uses process costing to establish the cost per unit of its output. The following information was available for the last month: Input units 10,000 Output units 9,850 Opening stock 300 units, 100% complete for materials and 70% complete for conversion costs Closing stock 450 units, 100% complete for materials and 30% complete for conversion costs. The company uses the weighted average method of valuing stock. What were the equivalent units for conversion costs?  
A 9,505 units  
B 9,715 units  
C 9,775 units  
D 9,985 units

3. A company uses process costing to value its output. The following was recorded for the period: Input materials 2,000 units at £4.50 per unit Conversion costs £13,340 Normal loss 5% of input valued at £3 per unit Actual loss 150 units. There were no opening or closing stocks. What was the valuation of one unit of output to one decimal place?  
A £11.8  
B £11.6  
C £11.2  
D £11.0

4. The following information relates to labour costs for the past month:  
Budget Labour rate £10 per hour Production time 15,000 hours Time per unit 3 hours Production units 5,000 units  
Actual Wages paid £176,000 Production 5,500 units Total hours worked 14,000 hours  
There was no idle time. What were the labour rate and efficiency variances?  
Rate variance Efficiency variance  
A £26,000 adverse £25,000 favourable  
B £26,000 adverse £10,000 favourable  
C £36,000 adverse £12,500 favourable  
D £36,000 adverse £25,000 favourable
